content.description:
# Revisit @ syntax: deref vs item-ref

In early Hob language design, it was decided that `@` would be item-ref syntax (resolve an item by name/uuid), not the Clojure-style deref reader macro. This made sense when Hob views were purely declarative hiccup — atoms were rare.

With the addition of DOM manipulation primitives (hiccup->dom+, dom-on!, dom-set-content!, etc.), atoms are now a core pattern for interactive views. Local mutable state via atoms appears in every interactive view: search inputs, filter state, debounce timers, etc. The symbol-browser-view has 6 deref calls; complex views like spatial-canvas will have many more.

Writing `(deref x)` everywhere is verbose and noisy. This is worth revisiting.

## Options

### 1. Keep status quo

`@` = item-ref, `(deref x)` for atoms.

- **Pro:** No breaking changes, unambiguous
- **Con:** Verbose for the now-common atom pattern

### 2. Flip it (recommended)

`@` = deref (Clojure convention). Give item-refs new syntax, e.g. `#item"name"` or a function `(item-ref "name")`.

- **Pro:** Short syntax for the high-frequency operation. Matches Clojure expectations. Item-refs are relatively rare.
- **Con:** Breaking change for existing Hob code that uses `@` for item-refs. Needs migration of existing views.

### 3. Eval-time dispatch

`@x` checks if `x` is an atom in scope → deref; otherwise → item-ref.

- **Pro:** No breaking change, both uses get short syntax
- **Con:** Muddies reader/evaluator boundary. Reader produces different AST depending on runtime state. Harder to reason about. Confusing error messages.

### 4. Add a new deref shorthand

Keep `@` for item-ref, add something like `$x` or `~x` for deref.

- **Pro:** No breaking change, both operations get short syntax
- **Con:** Invents non-standard syntax. `~` already used for unquote in quasiquote. `$` is unconventional for Lisps.

## Context

- Discovered during symbol-browser-view conversion: all `@atom` usages silently resolved as item-refs, returning truthy item-ref objects instead of atom values, causing search to always find 0 results
- Current workaround: use `(deref atom-name)` explicitly
- Frequency argument: atom deref appears ~6x per simple interactive view, item-ref appears ~0-2x per view
